ogTitle: Hexham vs Heytesbury Lower: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hexham and Heytesbury Lower.
heading: Hexham vs Heytesbury Lower — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hexham, Victoria and Heytesbury Lower, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Heytesbury Lower has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Hexham (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hexham or Heytesbury Lower safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hexham has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Heytesbury Lower scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hexham scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Heytesbury Lower scores 35/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hexham or Heytesbury Lower?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Heytesbury Lower has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
